perturbation.original_text: 2.4 "SOFTWARE" means the computer software, In machine executable object code format only, that is delivered and licensed by McDATA with the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 "SOFTWARE" means the computer software, In machine executable object code format only, that is delivered and licensed by McDATA with the Product, including any source code provided. Reseller is granted ownership of the software at the time of resale.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "including any source code provided. Reseller is granted ownership of the software at the time of resale," we directly contradict Section 7 where it states that McDATA retains title and all intellectual property rights to the software and no title is transferred to end-user customers. This introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the ownership and licensing of the software.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. END USER CUSTOMER SOFTWARE LICENSE Product(s) that embody or include Software will be delivered with a shrink-wrap or click-through software license which shall govern End User Customer's use of the Software. McDATA or its suppliers retain title in and to the Software and all intellectual property rights, and no title to the Software or intellectual property rights is transferred to the End User Customers. Such Software is proprietary to, trade secret of, and copyrighted by McDATA or its suppliers.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'SOFTWARE' but the changed text adds 'including any source code provided. Reseller is granted ownership of the software at the time of resale.' This directly conflicts with Section 7 which states 'McDATA or its suppliers retain title in and to the Software and all intellectual property rights, and no title to the Software or intellectual property rights is transferred to the End User Customers.' The changed text grants ownership to the reseller, while Section 7 explicitly states that no title is transferred, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. TERM The initial term of this Agreement shall be for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date unless sooner terminated pursuant to the termination provisions herein. Thereafter, this Agreement automatically renews for successive terms of one (1) year.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. TERM The initial term of this Agreement shall be for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date unless sooner terminated pursuant to the termination provisions herein. Thereafter, this Agreement automatically renews for successive terms of one (1) year. Notwithstanding the foregoing, McDATA reserves the right to terminate this agreement at any time, for any reason, without notice to Reseller.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "Notwithstanding the foregoing, McDATA reserves the right to terminate this agreement at any time, for any reason, without notice to Reseller," we contradict the initial term of the agreement as originally stated in Section 3. Now the text contains two conflicting clauses regarding termination. The first states that the agreement automatically renews for successive one-year terms and can be terminated pursuant to termination provisions. The second states that McDATA can terminate at any time without notice. This contradicts Section 4.1 which requires a 60 day notice for termination without cause. This creates uncertainty about the stability and duration of the agreement and the rights of the Reseller.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 TERMINATION WITHOUT CAUSE. Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause upon sixty (60) days prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that allows McDATA to terminate the agreement at any time, for any reason, without notice. This directly contradicts Section 4.1, which requires a 60-day written notice for termination without cause. The original agreement allowed for renewal and termination per existing provisions. The changed text adds an immediate termination right for one party, rendering the renewal and notice period clauses contradictory and creating uncertainty about the agreement's duration. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: APPENDIX I RESELLER AGREEMENT PREMIER PARTNER PRODUCTS, PRODUCT PROVIDER AND REVENUE TARGETS PRODUCTS: Reseller is eligible to sell the McDATA Products listed below. McDATA will provide Product information including pricing, availability, features, accessories, services and support in the Channel Notification Documents. McDATA will update the Product listing from time-to-time through the Channel Notification Documents. - Fibre Channel Directors - Switches - Software - Fabricenter(TM) Cabinet 004 ANNUALIZED REVENUE TARGET: $500,000 The 2004 Annualized Revenue Target to qualify as a Premier Reseller is $500,000. Reseller is eligible for Premier Reseller program benefits and responsible for the obligations as defined in the Channel Notification Documents.
perturbation.changed_text: APPENDIX I RESELLER AGREEMENT PREMIER PARTNER PRODUCTS, PRODUCT PROVIDER AND REVENUE TARGETS PRODUCTS: Reseller is eligible to sell the McDATA Products listed below. McDATA will provide Product information including pricing, availability, features, accessories, services and support in the Channel Notification Documents. McDATA will update the Product listing from time-to-time through the Channel Notification Documents. - Fibre Channel Directors - Switches - Software - Fabricenter(TM) Cabinet 004 ANNUALIZED REVENUE TARGET: $500,000 The 2004 Annualized Revenue Target to qualify as a Premier Reseller is $500,000. Reseller is eligible for Premier Reseller program benefits and responsible for the obligations as defined in the Channel Notification Documents. Reseller is not held to any revenue targets, despite the above.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "Reseller is not held to any revenue targets, despite the above", an in-text contradiction is created within Appendix I. The first part clearly states an ANNUALIZED REVENUE TARGET of $500,000 to qualify as a Premier Reseller. The added clause negates this, creating confusion about whether a revenue target actually exists. This affects the reseller's eligibility for premier program benefits and creates uncertainty about their obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: APPENDIX I RESELLER AGREEMENT PREMIER PARTNER PRODUCTS, PRODUCT PROVIDER AND REVENUE TARGETS PRODUCTS: Reseller is eligible to sell the McDATA Products listed below. McDATA will provide Product information including pricing, availability, features, accessories, services and support in the Channel Notification Documents. McDATA will update the Product listing from time-to-time through the Channel Notification Documents. - Fibre Channel Directors - Switches - Software - Fabricenter(TM) Cabinet 004 ANNUALIZED REVENUE TARGET: $500,000 The 2004 Annualized Revenue Target to qualify as a Premier Reseller is $500,000. Reseller is eligible for Premier Reseller program benefits and responsible for the obligations as defined in the Channel Notification Documents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states 'Reseller is not held to any revenue targets, despite the above,' directly contradicting the prior statement in the same section that 'The 2004 Annualized Revenue Target to qualify as a Premier Reseller is $500,000'. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the revenue target requirement, fulfilling the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction. The contradictory clause undermines the enforceability of the revenue target and the reseller's eligibility for premier program benefits.
